About Me

Who I am

Hi, I'm Harshita Kumari, a B.Tech CSE student at Birla Institute of Technology, Mesra. I blend code, creativity, and data to craft intelligent solutions — from AI-powered deep learning models to RAG-based teaching assistants.

My journey began with programming and web development, and has grown into a deep passion for artificial intelligence, computer vision, and data science. I interned at Tata Motors, Jamshedpur, where I built a U-Net semantic segmentation model for road extraction from satellite imagery, achieving strong IoU performance.

I believe in building scalable, impactful technology that not only solves problems but also makes a difference. When I'm not coding, you'll find me exploring new AI tools, solving problems on LeetCode, or leading initiatives through the Robotics Society and Drishti NGO.

I'm actively looking for internships, research collaborations, and opportunities to work on high-impact ML/AI projects. Let's build something great together!

Work Experience

Where I've worked

🏭
Data Science Intern
Tata Motors · Jamshedpur, Jharkhand
May 2025 – July 2025
On-site

Worked on the development of a deep learning model for automatic road extraction from satellite images. Implemented a U-Net architecture in PyTorch for pixel-level semantic segmentation using binary masks. This experience enhanced my knowledge of computer vision, model training pipelines, and practical application of deep learning in real-world geospatial problems.

  • Applied ML techniques and Python-based data processing to analyze and preprocess high-resolution image datasets, supporting downstream model training and evaluation workflows.
  • Implemented a U-Net CNN in PyTorch for pixel-level semantic segmentation; built a full pipeline including image normalization, mask alignment, and model performance evaluation using IoU metrics.
  • Gained hands-on experience with dataset handling, training pipelines, and visualization of predicted vs. ground-truth masks for qualitative and quantitative assessment.
PyTorchU-NetSemantic SegmentationPythonComputer VisionIoU MetricsOpenCV

Projects

Things I've built

🛰️
Road Extraction from Satellite Images

Implemented a U-Net CNN in PyTorch for pixel-level semantic segmentation to extract roads from high-resolution satellite imagery using binary masks. Built a full preprocessing and evaluation pipeline with IoU-based assessment and visual comparison of predicted vs. ground-truth masks for quantitative analysis.

PyTorchU-NetComputer VisionSemantic SegmentationIoUNumPy
🤖
RAG-Based AI Teaching Assistant

Built a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline that transforms lecture videos into searchable knowledge using Whisper for audio transcription, timestamped text chunking, and metadata processing. Implemented semantic embedding-based retrieval to fetch top-K relevant context chunks and supply them to an LLM via context-aware prompting, significantly reducing hallucinations and improving answer accuracy.

RAGWhisperLLMEmbeddingsNLPPython
